title: cardamine graeca var. eriocarpa
intro: Cardamine graeca var. eriocarpa, also known as the Greek bittercress, is a subspecies of the Cardamine graeca plant, belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits associated with Cardamine graeca var. eriocarpa include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Cardamine graeca var. eriocarpa can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
